original: State of Gojoseon (modern-day Korea) is founded by Dangun Wanggeom during the reign of Chinese Emperor Yao
Roman Emperor Theodosius makes a treaty with the Visigoths, granting them lands between the Lower Danube and the Balkans in return for military service
Cardinal Guido elected Pope Coelestinus II
William II of Holland elected Roman Catholic anti-king of Germany
Comet said to predict death of Pope Urban IV is last seen
Beauvais cathedral in France is completed (choir and transept), designed to be the largest French Gothic cathedral ever built
Dafydd ap Gruffydd, Prince of Gwynedd, Wales, becomes the first person executed by being hanged, drawn and quartered
Jews are expelled from Eger Bohemia
The Siege of Leiden is lifted by the Watergeuzen and the forces of Admiral Boisot

Sangley Rebellion: Overseas Chinese, known as the Sangley, rise up against Spanish authorities in Manila, Philippines. Spanish, local Japanese, and indigenous Tagalog forces massacre an estimated 20,000 ethnic Chinese.
French troops occupy Mardyke
Qing Dynasty naval commander Shi Lang reaches Taiwan (under the Kingdom of Tungning) to receive the formal surrender of Zheng Keshuang and Liu Guoxuan after the Battle of Penghu
An English and Dutch army occupies Limerick in Ireland
France and Holy Roman EmperorCharles VIsign peace accord
The Treaty of Nissa is signed by the Ottoman Empire and Russia at the end of the Russian-Turkish War, 1736-1739.
Washington proclaims 1st national Thanksgiving Day on Nov 26
Staedtler Company founded by J.S. Staedtler in Nuremberg, Germany
-10] Battle of Corinth, Mississippi
Skirmish at Bardstown, Kentucky
Bloomingdale's department store in NY opens
Modoc tribe Chief Kintpuash (also known as Captain Jack), the only Native American leader ever charged with war crimes, hanged by US Army at Fort Klamath, Oregon
New Zealand Natives, a privately organized and mainly Māori rugby team, play their first game in the UK; beat Surrey 4-1; first national rugby team to wear all-black and perform the 'haka'
Capt Guillaume of Kerckhoven marches into Boma Congo
J S Thurman patents motor-driven vacuum cleaner
France & Spain sign treaty for Morocco Independence
Mary McLeod Bethune opens Daytona Normal & Industrial School
US regime names Charles Magoon, governor of Cuba
William Vaughan Moody's "Great Divide" premieres in NYC
7.8 earthquake shakes Pleasant Valley, Nevada
Boris becomes king of Bulgaria
Brothers Edgar and Archibald Selwyn open Selwyn Theater at 229 W 42nd St, Times Square, NYC: now known as American Airlines Theatre
Serbian, Croatian and Slavic Parliament passes an accord for an 8 hr work day
American Pro Football Association (later the NFL) plays 1st full round of games; Dayton Triangles beat Columbus Panhandles, 14-0 in first official game at Triangle Park, Dayton
St Browns 1st baseman George Sisler collects his 257th hit of the season to set MLB record that lasts until the 21st century.; Browns beat White Sox, 16 - 7
First facsimile photo sent over city telephone lines, Washington, D.C.

Governor of Georgia names Progressive writer Rebecca Felton (87) to fill unexpired term in US Senate; she becomes 1st woman in Congess, oldest person (at the time), and shortest termed as she served only 24 hours in Novemberrgia Governor Hardwickbecomes 1st woman in Senate
King Hussein of Hedzjaz abdicts throne
1st congress of Pan European Movement (Pan-Europese Beweging) opens in Vienna
English long distance runner Violet Piercy sets first recognised female marathon record in 3:40:22 on the Polytechnic Marathon course between Windsor and London
French submarine "Ondine" sinks returning to Toulon, drowning 42
British Labour government recovers diplomatic relations with USSR
Kingdom of Serbs, Croats & Slovenes changes name to Yugoslavia
Iraq gains full independence from Britain, joins League of Nations
Gustave "Staf" de Clerq forms Flemish National Covenant
Italy invades Ethiopia, starting the Second Italo-Ethiopian War
Lemmer-Urk Dike closes
France Vichy government proclaims end to Jewish status
US forms parachute troops
French right-wing Mouvement Social Révolutionnaire (MSR) blow up 6 synagogues in Paris, with Nazi-provided explosives
Nazi SS kills all elderly Jewish men of Kerenchug, Ukraine
Launch of 1st A-4/V-2 rocket to altitude of 53 miles (85 km)
Triple Crown winner Whirlaway, ridden by George Woolf wins the Jockey Club Gold Cup at Belmont Park to become the first thoroughbred to amass more than $500,000 in lifetime earnings
British 8th army lands at Termoli, East Italy
Operations begin at PETA Java, defending (Japanese) fatherland
1st broadcast of Radio Herrijzend Netherland
RAF bombs West Kapelse
World Federation of Trade Unions forms; CIO a member
1st telescope lens 200" (508 cm) in diameter completed
WMAL (now WJLA) TV channel 7 in Washington, D.C. (ABC) begins
Columbia University reports discovery of uranium in Belgian Congo
WERD, 1st black-owned radio station, opens in Atlanta
Ethel Waters becomes 1st Black lead actress on TV sit-com (Beulah)
Indonesian army opens assault on Ambon, South Moluccas
CBS-TV airs the first coast-to-coast telecast of a prize fight; Australian light-heavyweight Dave Sands outpoints American Carl 'Bobo' Olson in 10 rounds at Chicago Stadium

Shot Heard 'Round the World': Bobby Thomson hits 3-run homer off Ralph Branca of the Brooklyn Dodgers, bottom 9th inning, 1 out, to give New York Giants dramatic 5-4 playoff win and NL pennant at the Polo Grounds
1st British nuclear test during Operation Hurricane at Monte Bello Island, Australia, UK becomes the world's third nuclear nation
1st video recording on magnetic tape in Los Angeles, California
KGGM TV channel 13 in Albuquerque, NM (CBS) begins broadcasting
"Father Knows Best" premieres
KFVS TV channel 12 in Cape Girardeau, MO (CBS) begins broadcasting
"The Mickey Mouse Club" television series premieres
Children's morning program "Captain Kangaroo" starring Bob Keeshan debuts on CBS-TV, beginiing a 29 year run - Good Morning, Captain!
Juscelino Kubitschek de Oliveira becomes President of Brazil
Soviet battleship "Novorossiisk" strikes WW II mine in Baltic Sea
WDBJ TV channel 7 in Roanoke, VA (CBS) begins broadcasting
WTVS TV channel 56 in Detroit, MI (PBS) begins broadcasting
Janio Quadros elected president of Brazil
New York Yankees beat rival Boston Red Sox, 8-7 at Yankee Stadium, ending season on 15 game winning streak & MLB record 193 HRs
San Francisco's White House department store is the 1st to accept BankAmericard
US sitcom "The Andy Griffith Show" premieres in CBS (runs till 1968)
KMED (now KTVL) TV channel 10 in Medford, OR (CBS) begins broadcasting
TV sitcom "Mr Ed" premieres on CBS
SF Giants (103-62) beat rivals LA Dodgers (102-63), 6-4 in season ending NL pennant decider; Dodgers set MLB record season attendance 2,755,184
Wally Schirra in Sigma 7 launched into Earth orbit
Hurricane Flora hits Haiti; about 5,000 die & 100,000 injured
NY Yankees beat Cleveland Indians, 8-3 to clinch Bronx Bombers 5th consecutive AL pennant, and 29th in club's history

Dual Melbourne Cup winning Australian jockey Pat Glennon rides French colt Sea Bird to victory in the Prix de l'Arc De Triomphe to claim an impressive double after the thoroughbred's English Derby win
Marshal Arturo da Costa e Silva elected president
Belgian King Boudouin inaugurates world's biggest floodgate in Antwerp
KGSC (now KICU) TV channel 36 in San Jose, CA (IND) begins
William J. "Pete" Knight sets highest speed ever recorded by a crewed, powered aircraft flying USAF/NASA's X-15 at 7,297 KPH/4,534 MPH/Mach 6.72
Howard Sacklers "Great White Hope" premieres in NYC
Military coup overthrows President Fernando Belaúnde Terry in Peru

The proposed civil rights march in Derry, Northern Ireland, is banned from the area of the city centre and the Waterside area; the banning order is issued under the Public Order Act by William Craig, then Home Affairs Minister
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
First umpires strike in MLB history lasts one day during League Championship Series; AL and NL presidents recognise newly-formed MLB Umpires Association; negotiate labor contract
WAPT TV channel 16 in Jackson, MS (ABC) begins broadcasting
Roric Harrison homers as Baltimore beats Cleveland, 4-3; last AL pitcher to homer until MLB inter-league play 25 years later
Spaceflight 71-2 launched; 1st flexible substrate photovoltaic flown
USSR performs nuclear test
Watergate criminal trial begins for five advisors and aides of President Nixon
Ireland AKZO director Tiede Herrema kidnap
Helmuth Kohl's CDU wins German parliament election
Gold hits record $223.50 an ounce in London
4 die in attack on a synagogue in Paris
Irish Nationalists at Maze Prison near Belfast end 7-month hunger strike
Milwaukee Brewers and Montreal Expos clinch their first-ever postseason appearances; Brewers beat Detroit, 2 - 1, for AL East title; Expos defeat NY Mets, 5 - 4, for NL East second playoff spot
Center fielder Robin Yount homers twice as Milwaukee whips Baltimore, 10-2 at Memorial Stadium to win the Brewers only AL East Championship
US government shuts down due to lack of agreement over passage of bills
21st Shuttle Mission (51J)-Atlantis 1-all-military flight launched
Falkland Islands (Islas Malvinas) adopts constitution
South Georgia & South Sandwich Islands adopts constitution
Walter Polovchak becomes a "free man" upon turning 18
Baltimore loses 6-3 to Detroit Tigers, assuring Orioles of club's first ever last-place finish in AL East standings
Soviet Yankee-class sub sinks off NC, 3 die
TASCC, a superconducting cyclotron at the Chalk River Laboratories, was officially opened.
Michael Pruffer of France skis 135.26 MPH at Portillo, Chile
San Diego catcher Benito Santiago's MLB rookie record 34-game hitting streak ends as Padres snap 9-game losing streak with a 1-0 victory v LA Dodgers
USSR performs underground nuclear test
26th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Discovery 7 returns to Earth after 4-day excursion
Criterion Center Theater opens at Broadway bet 44th & 45th Sts NYC
Lebanese kidnappers release Mithileshwar Singh (held for 30 months)
WBMW-FM, Washington, D.C. changes calls to WJFK and begins airing the Howard Stern Radio Show
Florida record store owner Charles Freeman is found guilty of obscenity, for selling records by the rap group "2 Live Crew"
MLB Atlanta Braves end season with 1-0 victory over San Diego Padres at Atlanta-Fulton County Stadium for franchise record 98th win (old record 95 in 1957, when they were based in Milwaukee)
Battle of Mogadishu: In an attempt to capture officials of warlord Mohamed Farrah Aidid's organization in Mogadishu, Somalia, 18 US Army Rangers and about 1,000 Somalis are killed in heavy fighting.
Cleveland Indians play their final MLB game at Cleveland Stadium; lose 4-0 to Chicago White Sox Chicago
Fernando Henrique Cardoso elected president of Brazil
1st first-class match at Hurstville Oval, Sydney (NSW vs Western Australia)
In a huge 3-way hockey trade of high profile NHL forwards, Claude Lemieux is sent to Colorado Avalanche, Steve Thomas to New Jersey Devils and Wendell Clark to the New York Islanders
Thunderdome in Tampa Bay renamed Tropicana Field
1st NHL game outside of North America: Vancouver Canucks beat Mighty Ducks of Anaheim, 3-2 in Tokyo, Japan
Gordie Howie (69) takes a shift with IHL'S Detroit Vipers, marking his 6th decade playing professional hockey
Japan's maglev train breaks world speed record for a manned train at 280.3 mph/451.1 kph
NHL Carolina Hurricanes lose in their 1st home game, 4-3 vs Pittsburgh Penguins at the Greensboro Coliseum in Greensboro, North Carolina

Chelsea scores massive upset when they humble English Premiership champions Manchester United, 5-0 at Stamford Bridge; Gus Poyet sets the tone scoring after just 27 seconds; Nicky Butt is sent-off for United
Roy Horn of Siegfried & Roy is attacked by one of the shows tigers, canceling the show for good.
Final game in Montreal Expos team history is played in NYC against the Mets at Shea Stadium, an 8-1 defeat; Jamey Carroll scores the final Expos run and Endy Chavez is the last Expos batter
Vercingetorix, leader of the Gauls, surrenders to the Romans underJulius Caesar, ending the siege and Battle of Alesia
FirstBattle of Philippi: TriumvirsMark AntonyandOctavianfight an indecisive battle with Caesar's assassinsBrutusandCassius
Vasco da Gama's Portuguese fleet attacks Calicut merchant pilgrim vessel the Miri on route from Mecca with 240 people on board, setting it on fire five days later
Battle of Moncontour: Royalist Catholic French forces led byHenry Duke of Anjoudefeat the Huguenots led byGaspard de Colignynear Poitiers, France
The Duke of Montrose issues a warrant for the arrest ofRob RoyMacGregor
CaptainJames Cookanchors at Alaska
A warrant is issued for the arrest of French mathematician and philosopherMarquis de Condorcetforcing him into hiding
Maya explorersJohn Lloyd StephensandFrederick Catherwooddepart New York on their first expedition to the Yucatan
American authorEdgar Allan Poeis found delirious in a gutter in Baltimore, Maryland under mysterious circumstances; it is the last time he is seen in public before his death.
US PresidentAbraham Lincolndesignates last Thursday in November as Thanksgiving Day
ExplorerFridtjof Nansenand his team complete first known crossing of Greenland interior, arriving in Godthaab
Edward Elgarand Cardinal Newman's oratorium "The Dream of Gerontius" premieres in Birmingham, England
New York Giants pitcherChristy Mathewsonstrikes out 16 Cardinals in a 3-1 Giants victory over St. Louis; new MLB record; finishes the game in 1 hour, 15 minutes
Pravda newspaper is founded byLeon Trotsky, Adolph Joffe, Matvey Skobelev, and other Russian exiles in Vienna
US Federal income tax signed into law (at 1%) by PresidentWoodrow Wilson
Tsar Ferdinand I of Bulgaria abdicates the throne in favour of his eldest son TsarBoris III
Julius Curtius succeedsGustav Stresemannas German foreign minister after the later passed away
William Walton's Viola Concerto premieres during the Proms at the Queen's Hall, London; composerPaul Hindemithis soloist with the Henry Wood Symphony Orchestra conducted by Walton
"The Maltese Falcon" directed by John Huston and based on Dashiell Hammett's 1929 novel of the same name, starringHumphrey BogartandMary Astor, premieres in New York City
Adolf Hitlersays Russia is "already broken and will never rise again" in a broadcast to the German people
FDRforms Office of Economic Stabilization
Allen Ginsberg's Howl and Other Poems is ruled not obscene
German statesmanWilly Brandtis elected mayor of West Berlin (1957-1966)
"Flair!", a daily radio program premieres on ABC radio network withDick Van Dykeas host
"The Dick Van Dyke Show" created byCarl Reiner, starringDick van DykeandMary Tyler Moorepremieres on CBS-TV
Cubs lose season finale 6-3 to Pirates;Ernie Banks-Don Kessinger combine to tie MLB record of 3 triple plays in a season
Whitey Fordnotches win #232 in season ending 11-5 victory v Boston Red Sox; becomes Yankees' winningest pitcher
American tennis starBillie Jean Kingbecomes first female athlete to win $100,000 in prize money in a single year; wins the $4,000 Virginia Slams Tournament in Phoenix, Arizona

US Formula 1 Grand Prix at Watkins Glen, New York: French driver François Cevert wins the race, his Tyrell-Ford teammate ScotsmanJackie Stewartfinishes to claim his second Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 29 points over Swede Ronnie Peterson
Future Baseball Hall of Fame left-handed pitcherSteve Carltonwins 27th game as Philadelphia beats Cubs, 11-1 at Wrigley Field; almost half Phillies 59 season wins
Willi Stoph succeedsWalter Ulbrichtas State Council of the German Democratic Republic (East Germany)
39-year oldFrank Robinsonbecomes MLB’s first African-American manager when he signs as player-manager for Cleveland Indians; agrees to 1-year contract estimated at $180,000
Future Basketball Hall of Fame guardJerry West(“Mr. Clutch”) retires after 14 NBA seasons with the LA Lakers; West has 25,192 career points; averages 29.1 ppg in 153 playoff games
Ted Bundyvictim Nancy Wilcox disappears in Salt Lake City, Utah
Apple Records releasesGeorge Harrison's sixth studio album, "Extra Texture" in UK
Future Baseball Hall of Fame right fielderHank Aaronsingles in his last MLB at-bat & drives in his 2,297th run as Milwaukee Brewers lose, 5-2 v Detroit Tigers
Indira Gandhiis arrested with four other members of her cabinet on corruption allegations, acquitted the next day
After a year's inactivity, American boxerMike Weaveroutpoints countryman James 'Quick' Tillis in 15 rounds in Chicago to retain his WBA heavyweight title
Parlophone releasesPaul McCartneyandMichael Jackson's duet single "Say, Say, Say" (from Paul's "Pipes of Peace" album) in UK
PopeJohn Paul IIbeatifies Dutch Roman Catholic priest and anti-Nazi activist Titus Brandsma (1881-1942); canonized as a saint in 2022
Darryl Sittler, Vladislav Tretiak, and Herbie Lewis are inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame as players, along with builders Alan Eagleson and Father David Bauer
Oakland Raiders promote 42-year-old Art Shell as the first African-American to be an NFL head coach in modern times; takes over fromMike Shanahan
Panamanian Defense Force attempted coup ofManuel Noriegafails
Detroit 1st basemanCecil Fielderbecomes 11th player in MLB history to hit 50 HRs in a season; 50th & 51st in 10-3 win v NY Yankees at Yankee Stadium

Kansas City 3rd basemanGeorge Brettbecomes first in MLB history to win a batting title in 3 different decades; goes 1 for 1 in Royals, 5-2 loss v Cleveland to win AL batting title with .329 average; AL batting champion 1976, 80
American "Queen of Pop" singerMadonnapremieres her "Erotica" music video on MTV
Irish pop singer Sinéad O'Connor rips up a picture of PopeJohn Paul IIon "Saturday Night Live"
Boris Yeltsindeclares state of emergency in Moscow
World music singerHarry Belafonteperforms in Tivoli Gardens in Copenhagen, Denmark
Gary Larson, announces he is retiring from doing "The Far Side" cartoon
Former NFL running back, broadcaster, and actorO.J. Simpsonis found not guilty of the murder of Nicole Simpson and Ron Goldman in Los Angeles, California
American writerDon DeLillopublishes "Underworld", his classic novel of twentieth-century America
St Louis 1st basemanMark McGwirehits his 65th homer of the season in a rain shortened 9-5 win over Cubs at Busch Stadium; wins 2nd straight HR title overSammy Sosa, who hits his 63rd HR in same game
TV series "Dark Angel" created byJames Cameronand Charles H. Eglee and starringJessica Albapremieres in the US

Warner Bros. Records releases "You're the One", the tenth solo studio album by singer-songwriterPaul Simon; Grammy nomination for Album of the Year makes Simon the first artist nominated in category in five consecutive decades (1960s-2000s)

After a 21-season career,Paul Coffeyofficially retires from the NHL, having won 4 Stanley Cups with Edmonton Oilers, finishing 2nd on all-time list for points by defensemen (1,531 in 1,409 games), and playing in 14 All-Star Games
San Diego outfielderRickey Hendersonscores a run in the 3rd inning of the Padres' 12-5 loss to LA Dodgers to tie the MLB record of 2,245 runs scored held by the legendary Ty Cobb
San Francisco Giants sluggerBarry BondsIs walked for the 171st time in 11-8 win at Houston; breaks Babe Ruth's 1923 MLB single-season record for walks
New England Patriots win their 18th consecutive NFL game, beating Buffalo Bills, 31-17 at Ralph Wilson Stadium;Tom Brady17-for-30 for 298 yards & 2 TDs

Elvis Presley's 1st public performance, at age of 10, singing "Old Shep" in youth talent contest at the Mississippi-Alabama Fair and Dairy Show, in Tupelo, Mississippi; contest is broadcast over WELO Radio, he wins fifth prize - $5.00 in fair ride tickets
Reunification of East Germany and West Germany; West German flag is raised above the Brandenburg Gate at the stroke of midnight
Battle at TV station Ostankino, Moscow townhall, about 25 killed
Saga, 52nd Emperor of Japan (809-23), born in Japan (d. 842)
